LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL ALLIANCE OF THEATRICAL STAGE EMPLOYEES, LOCAL 16'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. OTHER THAN THE FACT THAT THE REQUESTS AND TOPICS APPEAR TO BE NUMEROUS, THE MOVING PARTY HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF PROVIDING A SUFFICIENT FACTUAL BASIS FOR THE COURT TO ADDRESS WHETHER A PROTECTIVE ORDER SHOULD BE GRANTED AS TO ANY SPECIFIC ITEMS IN THE DEPOSITION NOTICE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE SCOPE OF THE NOTICE OF DEPOSITION AND ON A DATE OR DATES WHEN THE DEPOSITION WILL BE TAKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: MICHELLE DAWN BARBANTE, CSR # 12601 (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FRANK S. MOORE, TEL. NO. 292-6091 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JANNAH V. MANANSALA OF THE WEINBERG, ROGER & ROSENFELD, TEL. NO. (510) 337-1001 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
MICHELLE DAWN BARBANTE OF ATKINSON BAKER, TEL. NO. 421-3021, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT INTERNATIONAL ALLIANCE OF THEATRICAL STAGE EMPLOYEES, LOCAL 16'S Motion For A Protective Order is Denied. Other than the fact that the requests and topics appear to be numerous, the moving party has not met its burden of providing a sufficient factual basis for the court to address whether a protective order should be granted as to any specific items in the deposition notice and request for production of documents. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER ON THE SCOPE OF THE NOTICE OF DEPOSITION AND ON A DATE OR DATES WHEN THE DEPOSITION WILL BE TAKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Michelle Dawn Barbante, CSR # 12601
=(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STEPHAINE SCHEMPP's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted in part. Plaintiff has leave to plead a cause of action under Gov't 12946, amend the UCL cause of action, and add aiding and abetting allegations. The court denies plaintiff's request to plead a declaratory relief cause of action. A jury instruction should address the issue raised in the proposed declaratory relief cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
